title: Any quick way to restart ngrok link?
excerpt: Hi, I did the quick start project which worked very well (thanks for the good tutorial). But I’m revisiting my work the next day and did npm run start which is putting my project on an IP address instead of Ngrok. Any ti…

posts.author: tlane (Tyler Lane)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi, I did the quick start project which worked very well (thanks for the good tutorial). But I’m revisiting my work the next day and did npm run start which is putting my project on an IP address instead of Ngrok. Any tips to quickly generate a new ngrok link like the project does when you create it with npx @mondaydotcomorg/monday-cli scaffold run ./ quickstart-react?

posts.author: tlane (Tyler Lane)
posts.cookedHtml: Nevermind - found my answer in the example app documentation here for anyone else that got stuck on this: welcome-apps/apps/word-cloud at master · mondaycom/welcome-apps · GitHub

posts.author: VladMonday (Vlad Mystetskyi)
posts.cookedHtml:
@tlane Great catch! added additional note on how to find ngrok URL in the documentation itself

image1152×118 12.6 KB

posts.author: TMNXT-Dev (Ilja Zweigert)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @tlane ,

it is also possible to run your ngrok tunnel seperatly.

Monday APIv2 API-Token per User monday Apps & Developers
Hey @max91, the whole purpose of ngrok is expose your localhost to a public URL. In other words, monday.com gets a connection to the machine you are running ngrok on. First install ngrok. Then open your console and enter: ngrok http 3000 “HTTP” stands for the protocol you use and “3000” is the port you will expose by executing this command. Next up your console will display something like this: [image] The HTTPS URL is the one you want to copy and paste/use. Hope I could help you. …

By simple Ctrl + C you can cancel your connection and restart it with the ngrok command.

-Greetings

posts.author: system (system)
posts.cookedHtml: This topic was automatically closed 7 days after the last reply. New replies are no longer allowed.
